description: Assumptive 'ho' rests on background expectation rather than evidence specific to the present case. The inferential 'ke' (INFER) starts from particular signs. The form 'ho' gives the basis of the claim, while 'po' (POT) concerns the possibility of the event itself.
articulatory_notes: An open glottis lets breath form 'h' before the tongue draws back and the lips round for true-mid 'o'. Stress covers the monosyllable.
sound_symbolism: The breath-led onset and rounded finish give 'ho' a lightly held contour. That softness can serve as a mnemonic for assumption.
usage_notes: The evidential rank comes after voice and before modality. Future 'so' can combine with 'ho', but one clause cannot carry a second evidential source.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: When a claim concerns another person's state, 'ho' keeps the assumption open to that person's correction.
